paper_title: Model-based calculating tool for pollen-mediated gene flow frequencies in plants

methods.description: This method involves constructing a model-based tool to calculate pollen-mediated gene flow (PMGF) frequencies in plants using a quasimechanistic model. The model uses an inverse Gaussian function to describe pollen dispersal patterns and incorporates biological and climatic parameters.

steps.description: Select the quasimechanistic model described by Wang et al. (2016) to construct the PMGF calculating tool.
steps.searchable_summary: select and use the quasimechanistic model by Wang et al. (2016) for PMGF calculation

steps.description: Use an inverse Gaussian function to describe the pollen dispersal pattern of wind-pollinated plants.
steps.searchable_summary: implement inverse Gaussian function for pollen dispersal pattern

steps.description: Calculate the cumulative distribution function of the inverse Gaussian function based on three parameters: pollen diameter, pollen release height, and wind speed.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate CDF of inverse Gaussian using pollen diameter, release height, and wind speed

steps.description: Replace the exponential function in the model of Rong et al. (2010) with the function u to estimate PMGF frequencies.
steps.searchable_summary: replace exponential function with function u in Rong et al. (2010) model

steps.description: Measure five parameters: pollen diameter, pollen release height, outcrossing rate, crossability, and wind speed.
steps.searchable_summary: measure pollen diameter, release height, outcrossing rate, crossability, and wind speed

steps.description: Determine two variables: distance to recipient and threshold frequency.
steps.searchable_summary: determine distance to recipient and threshold frequency
